Filter Variants by Linkage Disequilibrium and Linear Dependence

Source: R/variant_processing.R
filter_variants_ld.Rd

Identifies pairs of variants with absolute correlation exceeding a threshold and removes one from each pair using a greedy algorithm. Optionally also removes linearly dependent columns via QR decomposition.

filter_variants_ld(
  G,
  ld_threshold = 0.9,
  remove_lindep = TRUE,
  prefer_keep = "lower_maf"
)

Arguments

G

Numeric matrix (n x p) of genotype dosage.

ld_threshold

Numeric correlation threshold. Pairs with |r| > ld_threshold are considered redundant. Default 0.9.

remove_lindep

Logical. If TRUE (default), also remove columns that are exact linear combinations of other columns after LD pruning. Uses QR decomposition. Prevents singularity in downstream Z-score computation and correlation matrices.

prefer_keep

Character controlling which variant to keep when breaking ties in LD pruning. One of:

  • "lower_maf" (default): keep the rarer variant

  • "higher_maf": keep the more common variant

  • "first": keep the earlier column (positional order)

Value

Integer vector of column indices to KEEP.

Examples

set.seed(42)
n <- 100
# Three variants: v1 and v2 highly correlated, v3 independent
v1 <- rbinom(n, 2, 0.1)
v2 <- v1  # perfect correlation
v2[1:3] <- 2 - v2[1:3]  # introduce small differences
v3 <- rbinom(n, 2, 0.05)
G <- cbind(v1, v2, v3)

keep <- filter_variants_ld(G, ld_threshold = 0.9)
keep  # one of v1/v2 removed, v3 kept
#> v1 v2 v3 
#>  1  2  3 
G_pruned <- G[, keep, drop = FALSE]

Contents
